Avena sativa, commonly known as oat or oat grass, is a type of flowering plant and a cereal grain. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits, which include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and mood swings. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa include avenacosides, avenanthramides, and avenalin, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Avena sativa include oat straw tea, oat grass tincture, and oat grass capsules, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
